title: workspaces — outbound customer webhooks
description: Outbound webhooks customers can subscribe to via the future webhook subscription UI. Distinct from `events.json` (internal Laravel event bus) and `broadcasts.json` (SPA-facing WebSocket channels): webhooks are HTTP POST calls to customer-configured URLs, signed with HMAC-SHA256 for authenticity. Every payload is a versioned envelope. Failed deliveries retry exponentially up to 8h, then dead-letter.

signing.description: Signature = hex(hmac_sha256(secret, timestamp + '.' + body)). Timestamp header defeats replay attacks.

envelope.shape: { event: string, version: integer, delivered_at: ISO-8601, workspace_id: string, application_id: string, data: <event-specific payload> }
envelope.description: Every event is versioned. Breaking payload changes bump `version`. Non-breaking additions (new fields) preserve the version.

webhooks.description: Fires after workspace provisioning transaction commits. Common downstream: CRM lead conversion, HRIS employee creation, analytics.

webhooks.payload_ref: { workspace: WorkspaceData, reason: string, suspended_by: PlatformUser | null }
{ workspace: WorkspaceData, archived_by: PlatformUser | null, hard_delete_scheduled_at: date-time }
webhooks.description: Includes the scheduled hard-delete timestamp so integrations can archive their side within the 30d window.

webhooks.payload_ref: { workspace: WorkspaceData, branding: BrandingData, before: object | null, after: object }
{ workspace: WorkspaceData, domain: DomainData, last_error: string }

v2Notes: Delivery subscription CRUD (customers configure their URLs + secrets) lives in a future `webhook` module. Workspaces publishes the event contract; the webhook module owns delivery.
